pullRequest.title: feat: use EntitySelector for light entity selection with search support
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

- Replace `cv.multi_select` with Home Assistant's `EntitySelector` for the lights field in the options flow
- This provides built-in search/typing functionality to find entities faster
- Better UX with HA's native entity picker interface
- Improved handling of renamed entities (addresses the issue in #1208 comment about being unable to edit groups after renaming lights)

## Changes

- Use `EntitySelector` with `EntitySelectorConfig(domain="light", multiple=True)` instead of `cv.multi_select`
- Simplified the validation logic (removed unused `light_options` dict and `all_lights_with_names`)
- Removed unused imports (`get_friendly_name`, `_supported_features`, `cv`)

Closes #1208

pullRequest.title: Fix infinite loop when disabling SimpleSwitch entities
pullRequest.body:
## Summary
- Fixes infinite loop that occurred when disabling SimpleSwitch child entities (Sleep Mode, Adapt Brightness, Adapt Color)
- Root cause: `SimpleSwitch._state` was initialized to `None` in `__init__`, but only set to a boolean in `async_added_to_hass()`. When entities are disabled, `async_added_to_hass()` is never called, so `_state` remained `None`
- The `_setup_listeners()` method has a `while` loop waiting for `_state is not None` for all SimpleSwitch children, causing infinite loop

## Test plan
- [x] Added regression tests that verify `SimpleSwitch._state` is not `None` after `__init__`
- [x] Tests fail before fix, pass after fix
- [x] Full test suite passes (130 tests)

Fixes #1264

pullRequest.title: Fix race condition where timer.start_time is None while is_running() is True
pullRequest.body:
## Summary
Fixes a race condition in `_AsyncSingleShotTimer` where `start_time` could be `None` while `is_running()` returns `True`.

## Problem
When `start()` creates a task with `asyncio.create_task()`, the task is **scheduled but not immediately executed**. This means:
- `is_running()` returns `True` (task exists and not done)
- But `start_time` is still `None` because `_run()` hasn't executed yet

This causes a `TypeError` when comparing `event.time_fired > timer.start_time`:

```
TypeError: '>' not supported between instances of 'datetime.datetime' and 'NoneType'
```

## Timeline of the Race
```
T0: start() called
    → self.task = asyncio.create_task(self._run())
    → Task is SCHEDULED but not yet executing
    → is_running() returns True (task exists, not done)
    → BUT start_time is still None!

T1: Event loop yields, _run() begins
    → self.start_time = dt_util.utcnow()  # NOW it's set
```

## Solution
Set `start_time` in `start()` **before** creating the task, ensuring it's always set when `is_running()` returns `True`.

## Test plan
- [x] Code review - the fix is straightforward
- [ ] Manual testing with auto-reset manual control timers

Fixes #1272

pullRequest.title: Check for external light mode (temperature vs rgb) switch
Fix propcache dependency (revert to functools)
pullRequest.body:
Fixes #1312

The `propcache` library was recently introduced but is not listed in `manifest.json` dependencies, causing `ModuleNotFoundError` in the simulator webapp (and potentially for some users).

This PR reverts the usage of `propcache.api.cached_property` back to the standard library `functools.cached_property`. This fixes the simulator and ensures the component remains self-contained without undeclared dependencies.

pullRequest.title: fix: reduce log verbosity for self-triggered off-to-on warning (#1433)
pullRequest.body:
Move full event object dump from warning to debug level in _off_to_on_state_event_is_from_turn_on() (switch.py:2717).

For lights with large effect_list attributes (e.g. Govee lights with 130+ effects), the warning dumped the entire Event object including both old_state and new_state, creating log entries thousands of characters long.

The warning now logs only entity_id and context.id, while the full event remains available at debug level for troubleshooting.

Fixes #1433
